skill.name: backend-models
skill.description: Guides creation and editing of Laravel Eloquent backend models, including relationships, attributes, casts, validation, scopes, factories, seeders, and model events.
skill.summary: Configure Laravel Eloquent models with consistent relationships, attributes, validation, and supporting model tooling.
skill.author: DevanB

security_audit.summary: The external command and weak cryptography alerts are false positives in Markdown prose and inline code formatting. The parent-directory reference at SKILL.md line 28 is a real packaging and filesystem concern because the skill delegates details to an unbundled file outside the skill directory.

medium_findings.title: Parent Directory Reference to Unbundled Guidance
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md line 28 links to ../../../agent-os/standards/backend/models.md. This traverses outside the skill directory and may cause the assistant to depend on unreviewed or unavailable repository content.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The traversal sequence is directly present in the Markdown link. It is not malicious by itself, but it creates a clear dependency on content outside the audited skill package.

low_findings.title: False Positive Static Matches in Markdown Text
low_findings.description: The alert for Ruby or shell backtick execution at SKILL.md line 12 is inline Markdown for app/Models/. The weak cryptography alerts at lines 3 and 8 appear to match ordinary model-related prose, with no cryptographic operation present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain Markdown front matter, prose, and inline code formatting only. I found no command execution syntax or weak cryptographic API usage in the scanned file.

dangerous_patterns.title: Relative Path Traversal in Documentation Link
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill references a parent-directory path instead of bundling the required model standard. This can pull review scope outside the marketplace package.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The path traversal sequence is explicit on the cited line. The risk is contextual because it is a Markdown link, not executable filesystem code.

content.value_statement: Backend model changes often need consistent relationships, attributes, timestamps, and validation rules. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Laravel Eloquent model work with focused model conventions.
actual_capabilities: Guides edits to model files in app/Models/ or similar model directories.
Covers Eloquent relationships such as hasMany, belongsTo, belongsToMany, hasOne, and morphTo.
Supports model attributes, casts, fillable fields, hidden fields, accessors, and mutators.
Applies guidance for timestamps, scopes, soft deletes, traits, events, and observers.
Includes model factory and seeder considerations for testing and sample data.
limitations: The detailed standards file is referenced outside the skill package and is not bundled.
The guidance is focused on Laravel Eloquent and may not fit other ORMs.
It does not execute migrations, database checks, or test suites by itself.
It provides conventions but does not replace project-specific architecture review.

use_cases.title: Create a New Domain Model
use_cases.description: Define relationships, attributes, casts, timestamps, and validation expectations for a new Laravel model.

use_cases.title: Review Existing Model Configuration
use_cases.description: Check an existing model for missing relationships, unsafe fillable settings, weak casts, or inconsistent scopes.

use_cases.title: Prepare Test Data Support
use_cases.description: Plan model factories and seeders that match the model relationships and expected field types.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the backend-models skill to create a Laravel Eloquent model for [entity]. Include fillable fields, casts, timestamps, and basic relationships.

prompt_templates.title: Add Relationships
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the backend-models skill to update [model name] with the correct Eloquent relationships for [related models]. Explain each relationship choice.

prompt_templates.title: Review Model Safety
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the backend-models skill to review [model file] for fillable fields, hidden attributes, casts, validation logic, scopes, and event behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Design Model Layer
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the backend-models skill to design the model layer for [feature]. Include relationships, constraints, scopes, factories, seeders, and observer recommendations.

output_examples.input: Create a Product model for a Laravel catalog.
output_examples.output: A model plan covering product attributes, price casting, category relationships, timestamp handling, fillable fields, and factory requirements.

output_examples.input: Review a User model with roles and profile data.
output: Relationship recommendations for roles and profile records.
Notes on hidden attributes, fillable fields, casts, scopes, and model events.
Follow-up checks for factories, seeders, and validation ownership.

output_examples.input: Plan backend models for an order workflow.
output_examples.output: A domain model outline for orders, items, customers, payments, and status scopes, including relationship and factory guidance.

best_practices: Review the project model conventions before applying generated changes.
Keep relationship names, foreign keys, and casts aligned with the database schema.
Run relevant model, factory, and seeder tests after implementation.
anti_patterns: Do not copy relationship patterns without checking the actual schema.
Do not expose sensitive fields through fillable attributes or serialized output.
Do not depend on the unbundled parent-directory standards file in marketplace packaging.

faq.question: What frameworks does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets Laravel Eloquent model work, based on the model terms listed in SKILL.md.

faq.question: Can it create database migrations?
faq.answer: It can inform model-related schema expectations, but the skill is focused on models rather than migration execution.

faq.question: Does it support factories and seeders?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes guidance for model factories and database seeders that use models.

faq.question: Is the referenced standards file included?
faq.answer: No. SKILL.md links to a parent-directory standards file that is not included in the scanned package.

faq.question: Is this safe to use in Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: It is safe to publish with a warning. Users should review the external relative link before relying on the skill.

faq.question: What should users verify after using it?
faq.answer: Users should verify schema alignment, relationship behavior, fillable fields, casts, hidden attributes, factories, seeders, and tests.
